---
title: "Interactive Teaching Document"
subtitle: "Tabsets, callouts and live widgets — the pattern behind your learning apps"
---

This mirrors your single-file HTML learning apps, but authored in Markdown and
fully reproducible.

## Layered content with tabsets

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Concept
Emicizumab is a bispecific antibody bridging activated factor IX and factor X,
mimicking the cofactor function of **factor VIII**.

### Mechanism
It does **not** require activation and is not affected by FVIII inhibitors —
hence its role in haemophilia A with inhibitors.

### Monitoring
Standard APTT-based assays are unreliable on emicizumab. Use a
**bovine-based chromogenic** FVIII assay to monitor.

:::

## Callouts for emphasis

::: {.callout-warning}
## Assay pitfall
Emicizumab shortens the APTT dramatically and invalidates one-stage FVIII assays.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Expand: dosing summary
Loading 3 mg/kg weekly ×4, then a maintenance schedule (weekly / fortnightly /
four-weekly). *Verify against the current SmPC before clinical use.*
:::

## A live interactive: dose–exposure explorer

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof weight = Inputs.range([10, 120], {value: 70, step: 1, label: "Body weight (kg)"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
loading = 3.0 * weight
md`A **3 mg/kg** loading dose for a **${weight} kg** patient is
**${loading.toFixed(0)} mg** per weekly dose during the 4-week loading phase.`
```
